figures.caption: Table 3: Natural correspondence between HHD components and modality classes. Each modality class contributes most directly to one geometric mode of risk; the contribution is not exclusive but reflects the structural role of each data type. To the best of our knowledge, no prior framework establishes this correspondence (cf. [6, 7, 8]).
Table 2: HHD components with mechanistic interpretation and intervention strategy.
Table 5: HHD components and their operational intervention targets.
Table 1: Expert decomposition of Fθ aligned with simplicial UAT requirements.

Table 4: Extended comparison of GVF against representative baseline classes. ✓ = supported by construction; ◦ = partial or heuristic; × = not supported. Complexity: |V | nodes, |K1| edges, |K2| 2-simplices, m output dimension, d scalar hidden size. All comparisons are architectural, not empirical.
